channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Graffiti, 3rd July
item.description:
New graffiti on footbridge, ramps, stairs and approaches

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Tunnel Avenue, Greenwich
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Man dumping litter in the streets, 3rd July
item.description:
At 6:30pm on Friday 3rd July I saw a white man in his 60s get into his car with his dog. As he got in the drivers door he threw a bag of donuts into the gutter and then drove off even though he could see I was taking photos. He was driving a black Mercedes motor car KS13 UKU, he had been parked in Empress Drive by the side of number 97.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Empress Drive, Chislehurst, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: In front of emergency exit, 3rd July
item.description:
Way too many bikes on road not parked in designated areas bikes being pushed over like dominoes lime bike employees delivering and collecting bikes after midnight so much noise drunk people using them and crashing into parked cars causing damage

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 41 -80, Sylvester Road, Hackney, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Holyoake Footpath, 3rd July
item.description:
Table fly tipped amongst the weeds and nettles against the wall on Holyoake footpath

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Holyoake Footpath, Rotherhithe, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Old rug and bin bag left, 3rd July
item.description:
Old rug and bin bags have dumped

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Springwater Close, Plumstead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: It's fly tipping on estate land, 3rd July
item.description:
Now it's on estate land will the waste of space residents officer for four squares actually do something about it.... Nah it does not bother them

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Drummond Road, Bermondsey, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Hedge obstructing path (overgrown), 3rd July
item.description:
Needs cutting so you can get past

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Hollydale Road, Nunhead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Homeless sleeping inside playground unit, 3rd July
item.description:
Rubbish, sleeping bag, and bags with unknown contents left by homeless on top of play unit at Hackney Downs playground. This is unacceptable, please remove asap. I reported it yesterday and this evening everything remains in there. Children playing ,unsaf.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Downs Park Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Leaning Tree, 3rd July
item.description:
It’s evident previous attempts to support this tree into an upright position have failed. Could renewed effort be made?

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Mcleod Road, Abbey Wood
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Multi Sports Court, 3rd July
item.description:
Grass is growing through the tarmac. Dogs pee on it. It is a safety hazard. Could it be fixed / made safe?

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Finchale Road, Abbey Wood
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Broken bed on footpath, 3rd July
item.description:
Owners of 4 Irvine way have put a broken bed outside their house on pavement,could potentially trip someone up.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Irvine Way, Crofton, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Unlevel surface, 3rd July
item.description:
Path is unlevel impacting wheelchair users and anyone with insecure footing.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Finchale Road, Abbey Wood
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Weeds in kerbs all down the road, 3rd July
item.description:
Looks an eyesore
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Cray Road, Lessness Heath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Overgrown trees, 3rd July
item.description:
Trees are overgrown and hanging into footpath,have to bend down to avoid getting your eyes poked out.been like this since April.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Irvine Way, Crofton, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Grads tree outside, 3rd July
item.description:
Dear Council, I would like to report that the grass and trees outside our home have become severely overgrown. There are many insects and bugs living in the area, and some of them bite children, making it unsafe for them to play outside. I kindly request that the grass and trees be cut back as soon as possible for the safety of local residents.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Glimpsing Green, Upper Belvedere, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Works barrier left at top of Cray Road. Been there a few weeks, 3rd July
item.description:
Eyesore and trip hazard
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Cray Road, Lessness Heath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fridge, cooker and litter opposite school blocking the path, 3rd July
item.description:
A landlord to a property has tripped all this rubbish outside. It was all over the pavement and I couldn’t walk with the buggy I had to walk in the main road. I moved the stuff away as it is blocking the entrance to my home. This is a consistent issue. It happened last month and I reported it, nothing happened the stuff reminded there and kept blocking my home. I had to beg a council man to take it away as people kept going through the stuff. It is getting so bad that I need to leave my baby in the buggy far away from me so I can move the stuff from blocking my entrance. It’s a health and safety issue and a safety issue at the fact I have to leave the buggy on the other side of the pavement or walk in the main road to remove the stuff

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 22, Valmar Road, Camberwell, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rubbish from shops all the way down Vray Road, 3rd July
item.description:
Eyesore
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Cray Road, Lessness Heath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Double Parking, 3rd July
item.description:
The part of Oakfield Road, between the two Meaford Way entrances, has become very dangerous for me, an elderly e-bike rider, because of the double parking and volume of traffic. I live in Woodbine Grove and use my bike more as a mobility vehicle than anything. I cannot use the footpath, and my only alternative is to ride around Meaford Way, but then the traffic stops me getting out at the other end. I have difficulty walking to the corner shop, so is it possible to widen the road between these two points now that a factory has been demolished?
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Oakfield Road, Anerley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Ejecta from potholes: loose chippings and grit on Junction, 3rd July
item.description:
Loose chippings and grit covering the road at a downhill Give Way junction. Dangerous to all vehicles, potentially lethal to cyclists and motor cyclists. Village Way is not being effectively swept and hasn't been. for years.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 1, Uplands, Beckenham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
